mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following rate decreases are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or Rank IC is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month contraction in industrial production and retail sales amplifies recession risk beyond the independent effect of either series, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and RSAFS fails to produce negative 20-day returns in at least 60% of joint-contraction months, or the interaction term adds less than 0.01 incremental R-squared.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2%, month-over-month increases in consumer prices signal inflationary pressure that forces tighter monetary policy, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During FEDFUNDS > 2% regimes, month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in industrial production reflects contracting manufacturing activity that reduces corporate revenue growth, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of months, or Rank IC turns positive over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,200 thousand units signals a collapse in construction and durable goods demand that precedes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST below 1,200 is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered months, or the signal's Rank IC exceeds zero over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with falling retail sales creates a negative wealth-and-income feedback loop that is stronger than either variable alone, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising UNRATE and falling RSAFS fails to produce negative 5-day SPX returns in at least 55% of concurrent observations.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth is negative, accelerating consumer price inflation squeezes real earnings and corporate margins, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In quarters with GDPC1 contraction, CPIAUCSL acceleration shows no negative correlation with 20-day SPX returns (Spearman rank correlation > -0.05).
